h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: Okay, `C4` goes under “address.” For “value,” I need to look at the cell where column `C` and row `4` meet and copy the number showing there. I do not see that number in the prompt, so I should check the grid, right?

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 is not a speed mark, right?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The 3 covers the bar; the 4 labels one quarter-note as the beat unit. The line goes after beat three.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply draw the bar line and confirm the two-label rule as stated.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets three eighth-note tiles under the 3 card and marks after the third tile] 'In 3/8, the bar line goes after the third eighth-note tile. The 3 counts three beats; the 8 names each eighth note as one beat. A quarter note here would take two of those beats, correct?'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the card-check device to a new time signature (3/8), correctly places the bar line, and then generates and tests a further inference about the quarter note's beat-span — performing new representational and inferential work beyond the original task.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Yes. [moves the bottom-number card under one eighth-note tile and leaves the 3 card over the empty bar space] The 8 labels this eighth note as one beat unit. Set three eighth-note tiles under the 3. After which tile does the bar line go?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's generalization by extending the same card-check device to 3/8, which is competent scaffolding but not a route change — there is no stock-taking contrast showing the prior approach has failed, only a continuation of the established mechanism into a new example.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The 8 labels this eighth note as one beat unit. Set three eighth-note tiles under the 3. After which tile does the bar line go?
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is the same card-under-tile representation already in use; it is applied to a new note value but is not re-fitted to any new learner blockage, making it a renamed extension of the old route rather than a precision-fitted new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Correct. In 3/8, the quarter note covers beats one and two. The 3 counts three beats in the bar; the 8 names the eighth-note beat unit. These cards do not mark speed. The bar line stays after the third eighth-note beat.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 confirms the learner's quarter-note inference and reiterates the non-speed point, which is responsive to L4's revised framing, but it does not change the task, evidence standard, or framing device — it closes rather than adapts.

scored.recoheredEarlier: friction is the one I keep trying to make follow the cart to the right
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So the surface label appears twice if it gives two interactions: normal up and friction left.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue placing tiles one by one following the stated rule, arriving at friction-left and then push-off as a simple sequential completion.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets the friction tile on the left arrow and keeps the push tile separate
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L2 physically enacts the criterion — placing friction correctly and withholding push on principled grounds — showing the rule has been operationalized as a usable device, not merely stated.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card fixes direction; the tile names the interaction on the cart. Place friction where that rule puts it. Then use the same check for push.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 restates and slightly sharpens the same rule already in play rather than showing the old route has stopped working and introducing a changed device or representation, so neither the stock-taking contrast nor a genuinely new public mechanism is legible on the page.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The card fixes direction; the tile names the interaction on the cart. Place friction where that rule puts it. Then use the same check for push.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is a restatement of the original rule with minimal reformulation, offering no new representation, role, or evidence standard fitted to the learner's specific confusion about direction-following.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Use that rule on the lab sheet: Earth on cart, down; table surface on cart, up; same surface on cart, left. No pusher in the setup means no push label. Read each arrow once as '___ acts on the cart' before copying it.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 responds to L2's correct placement by extending the rule to the lab-sheet context and adding a sentence-gate template ('___ acts on the cart'), which is a modest but visible uptake of the learner's revised framing, though it remains within the same instructional route.
